nodes.label: AI as assistant for verification and testing
nodes.label_negative: AI as autonomous agent taking full control
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI as almost an assistant - checking my work, ensuring it is high-quality in terms of style + linting rules, as well as writing tests

nodes.label: AI as tool to cover blind spots and biases
nodes.label_negative: Relying solely on own perspective without AI
nodes.grounding_span: It's mostly to cover off my own biases and blind spots I suppose

nodes.label: AI reliability in debugging complex errors
nodes.label_negative: AI getting stuck in loops and failing to fix issues
nodes.grounding_span: it rewrote my code several times, getting caught in a loop of trying things it had already tried

nodes.label: Human control over implementation
nodes.label_negative: AI taking full control of code changes
nodes.grounding_span: When implementing features, I prefer to stay 'in control'

nodes.label: AI integration in development environment
nodes.grounding_span: I really like the integrated nature of Cursor, and I can pick and choose models as I see fit

nodes.label: AI as threat to foundational coding skills
nodes.label_negative: AI preserving and enhancing human skills
nodes.grounding_span: I fear that this may lead to a generation/cohort of engineers who do not truly understand how to code

nodes.label: Balance between AI and human work
nodes.label_negative: Complete AI takeover or no AI use
nodes.grounding_span: I think the answer lies with a balance between AI and humans

nodes.label: Professional competence and code quality
nodes.grounding_span: ensuring it is high-quality in terms of style + linting rules

nodes.label: Industry sustainability and skill preservation
nodes.grounding_span: I fear that this may lead to a generation/cohort of engineers who do not truly understand how to code

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as assistant for verification
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI as almost an assistant - checking my work, ensuring it is high-quality

nodes.label: Frustration with AI failing at debugging
nodes.grounding_span: This was particularly frustrating to watch in real-time

nodes.label: Concern about AI replacing human skills
nodes.grounding_span: I fear that this may lead to a generation/cohort of engineers who do not truly understand how to code

nodes.label: Optimism about future trust in AI
nodes.grounding_span: I will learn (or, have to learn) to trust the AI with more complex tasks

nodes.label: Delegation of verification tasks while retaining control of implementation
nodes.grounding_span: I often will write implementation code, then delegate to AI to write tests, or to check my assumptions

nodes.label: Concern-driven future planning about industry evolution
nodes.grounding_span: I think the role will evolve to be more typically managerial
